name: Prassopitta (Leeks in Phyllo Pastry)
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the leeks by discarding the green parts and leaves; use only the white part.
2. Cut into quarters, then chop into small pieces; place in a strainer and wash under running water for several minutes.
3. Melt the butter in a pot. Add the leeks, cook until they soften and the water has evaporated. Cool.
4. Beat the eggs in a bowl until light. Put the leeks into another bowl, and add the farina, cheese, salt, and pepper; mix well.
5. Butter a pan the size of the phyllo sheets.
6. Place 4 to 5 sheets of phyllo on the bottom of the pan, brushing each with melted butter before adding it.
7. Spread a layer of leeks on the phyllo and top it with about 3 tablespoons of beaten egg.
8. Spread 2 sheets of phyllo, again brushing each one with butter. Spread another layer of leeks and top with more egg.
9. Continue layering in the leeks and eggs in this manner until all are spread in the pan.
10. Spread the last 4 sheets of phyllo carefully over the top layer, brushing each one with butter.
11. Pour any remaining butter over the top.
12. Score the phyllo with a sharp knife into serving-size pieces.
13. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for about 30 minutes.
